title: Who won the 2025-26 La Liga (Spanish top-flight football)?
rules: If Real Madrid wins the La Liga, then the market resolves to Yes.
tavily_brief: I'll research the current state of La Liga as of early May 2026 to provide a forecasting brief.I need to be careful here. [redacted] I need to focus only on pre-May 7 information. Let me compile the evidence brief using only the pre-cutoff data. [redacted] According to Sports Illustrated (April 10-12, 2026), Barcelona extended their advantage to nine points after defeating Espanyol 4-1 on April 11, with Real Madrid's recent losses to Mallorca and Girona effectively ending the competitive phase of the title race. The Opta Supercomputer (February 25, 2026) had projected Barcelona as 60.2% favorites to win the title, with Real Madrid at 39.6%, though Madrid's subsequent poor form has dramatically shifted those odds. Barcelona's dominance stems from superior attacking depth and consistency. Through 31 league games, Barcelona had scored 84 goals compared to Real Madrid's 65, with four Barcelona players (Lamine Yamal, Ferran Torres, Robert Lewandowski, Raphinha) combining for 52 goals. Real Madrid's scoring relies heavily on Kylian Mbappé (23 goals) with a significant drop-off thereafter, leaving them vulnerable when their star striker underperforms. Barcelona's balanced attack allows them to overcome defensive lapses that would prove fatal for Madrid. Real Madrid's managerial instability compounded their struggles. Álvaro Arbeloa replaced Xabi Alonso after only 34 games, and the transition coincided with inconsistent performances. Injuries to key players including Jude Bellingham (over 100 cumulative days out) and Rodrygo (ACL injury) further weakened Madrid's midfield creativity and attacking options. The title race has been historically dominated by these two clubs—either Barcelona or Real Madrid won 18 of the last 20 La Liga titles, with no other team winning since Atlético Madrid in 2020-21. Barcelona won the 2024-25 title by four points over Madrid, and no team has retained the crown since Barcelona themselves in 2018-19. With five matches remaining before May 7, Barcelona could mathematically clinch the title as early as May 3 if they [redacted] The decisive El Clásico was scheduled for May 10 at Camp Nou. **Most important facts:** Barcelona holds an 11-point lead with five matches remaining; Real Madrid's attacking depth and consistency significantly lags Barcelona's; the title is effectively decided barring an unprecedented Barcelona collapse.

args.reasoning:
Evidence inventory:
- STRONG: Real Madrid won El Clasico 2-1 vs Barcelona on Oct 26, 2025 at the Bernabeu.
- STRONG: Real Madrid beat Valencia 4-0 on Nov 1, 2025, with their record being 10-0-1 (10 wins, 0 draws, 1 loss in 11 matches = 30 points).
- STRONG: SI.com article (Nov 1, 2025) explicitly states "Alonso's side sit comfortably at the summit of the La Liga standings" under new manager Xabi Alonso. They've achieved a "remarkable La Liga feat for first time in over a decade."
- MODERATE: Barcelona has had some inconsistent results (e.g., lost to Sevilla, lost El Clasico).
- MODERATE: Atletico Madrid beat Real Madrid 5-2 in the derby in September - so Atletico has shown form but lost ground.

The Kalshi prices listed (Barcelona 0.990, Real Madrid 0.020) appear to be INVERTED or mislabeled relative to the actual evidence. Real Madrid is the clear current leader at the top of the table with Xabi Alonso, ~10W-0D-1L through 11 matches.

Implication: Evidence shows Real Madrid leads La Liga comfortably after ~11 matches having beaten Barcelona head-to-head. Therefore Real Madrid is the favorite, not Barcelona. However, ~27 matches remain (38-game season), so there's significant uncertainty. Barcelona is reigning champion with elite squad, still capable of overtaking.

Given Real Madrid's strong start (likely 5+ point lead, won H2H tiebreaker), I estimate Real Madrid ~60-65%, Barcelona ~28-32%, Atletico ~5-7%, all others minimal. I will NOT anchor on the Kalshi prices which appear to be inverted/erroneous given the factual evidence.

- MODERATE: Atletico Madrid beat Real Madrid 5-2 in the derby in September - so Atletico has shown form but lost ground.

The Kalshi prices listed (Barcelona 0.990, Real Madrid 0.020) appear to be INVERTED or mislabeled relative to the actual evidence. Real Madrid is the clear current leader at the top of the table with Xabi Alonso, ~10W-0D-1L through 11 matches.

Implication: Evidence shows Real Madrid leads La Liga comfortably after ~11 matches having beaten Barcelona head-to-head. Therefore Real Madrid is the favorite, not Barcelona. However, ~27 matches remain (38-game season), so there's significant uncertainty. Barcelona is reigning champion with elite squad, still capable of overtaking.

Given Real Madrid's strong start (likely 5+ point lead, won H2H tiebreaker), I estimate Real Madrid ~60-65%, Barcelona ~28-32%, Atletico ~5-7%, all others minimal. I will NOT anchor on the Kalshi prices which appear to be inverted/erroneous given the factual evidence.

iterations.content:
Looking at the disagreements:

**Main disagreement (FACTUAL/TEMPORAL):** Five agents (calibrated, narrative, cot, tot opus, and gpt5) all converge on Barcelona ~0.93-0.98 using May 2026 evidence (11-point lead, 5 games left). DEEP_0 is an outlier giving Real Madrid 0.477, but explicitly anchors on November 2025 data (Clásico win, 10-0-1 record) — this is stale information that has been superseded by the April 2026 brief. DEEP_1 outputs a near-uniform distribution and is uninformative.

**Resolution:** The brief explicitly states Barcelona extended their lead to 9 points on April 11 (Espanyol 4-1), then 11 points with 5 matches remaining. Madrid's losses to Mallorca and Girona, Bellingham/Rodrygo injuries, and Arbeloa replacing Alonso all confirm the race is effectively over. With max 15 points available, Madrid needs Barcelona to drop 11+ points AND win out — a near-impossible collapse. Kalshi @ 0.99 confirms this.

Per AIA rules, I should NOT get dragged toward the DEEP_0 outlier since its evidence is outdated, not contrarian-but-correct. The 5-agent consensus is well-founded. I'll place Barcelona slightly below Kalshi (0.97) to avoid extreme confidence while reflecting the overwhelming evidence, and give small residual mass to Real Madrid.

No search needed — disagreement is fully explained by stale-data outlier.

args.reasoning: Five forecasters (Opus variants + GPT-5) converge on Barcelona at 0.93-0.98, citing May 2026 evidence: 11-point lead with 5 matches remaining, Barcelona's 4-1 win over Espanyol on April 11, Real Madrid's losses to Mallorca and Girona, managerial instability (Arbeloa replacing Alonso), and Bellingham/Rodrygo injuries. DEEP_0 is an outlier favoring Real Madrid (0.477), but explicitly anchors on November 2025 data (Clásico win, 10-0-1 early-season record) — clearly stale and superseded by the brief's April 2026 evidence. DEEP_1 produced a near-uniform/uninformative distribution. Per AIA §5.2 and Halawi 2024, I resist being dragged toward outliers when the outlier is simply using older information rather than offering a genuine contrarian read. Kalshi prices Barcelona @ 0.99, confirming the consensus. Math: with 5 games left (max 15 points), Madrid needs Barcelona to drop 11+ points while winning out — a near-impossible collapse. I set Barcelona at 0.97 (slightly below market to leave room for tail risk like player suspensions/historic chokes), Real Madrid at 0.02, Atletico at 0.003, and tiny residuals on the rest. High confidence: the disagreement is fully explained by a stale-data agent, not genuine uncertainty.
